Question Text: You are observing a piece of tissue from an unknown organism. Which of the following observations would convince you that you are looking at animal tissue?
Option 1: Microscopic analysis reveals that individual cells have cell walls.
Option 2: Microscopic analysis reveals the presence of nerve cells.
Option 3: Biochemical analysis indicates that the cells have photosynthetic capability.
Option 4: Observation of either nerve cells or cell walls is an indication that the tissue comes from an animal.
Option 5: Any of these observations are indications that the tissue comes from an animal.

Question Text: Imagine you are on the way to Tahiti for a vacation but your plane crash-lands on a previously undiscovered island. You soon find that the island is teeming with unfamiliar organisms and you decide to survey them. You select three organisms and observe them in detail. Which organism would you classify as an animal?

Use the following information to answer the question below.

Trichoplax adhaerens (Tp) is the only living species in the phylum Placozoa. Individuals are about 1 mm wide and only 27 ?m high, are irregularly shaped, and consist of a total of about 2000 cells, which are diploid (2n = 12). There are four types of cells, none of which are nerve or muscle cells, and none of which have cell walls. They move using cilia, and any edge can lead.
 Tp feeds on marine microbes, mostly unicellular green algae, by crawling atop the algae and trapping it between its ventral surface and the substrate. Enzymes are then secreted onto the algae, and the resulting nutrients are absorbed. Tp sperm cells have never been observed, nor have embryos past the 64-cell (blastula) stage.

Which of the following Tp traits is different from those of most other animals?
Option 2: Tp digests food outside its body first and then absorbs resulting nutrients.

Question Text: Both animals and fungi are heterotrophic. Animal heterotrophy can be distinguished from fungal heterotrophy in that most animals derive their nutrition by __.
Option 3: consuming living rather than dead organisms
Option 4: using enzymes to digest their food

Question Text: Which of the following statements concerning animal taxonomy is (are) TRUE? 1. Animals are more closely related to plants than to fungi. 2. All animal clades based on body plan have been found to be incorrect. 3. Kingdom Animalia is monophyletic. 4. Animals only reproduce sexually. 5. Animals are thought to have evolved from flagellated protists similar to modern choanoflagellates.
During the Cambrian explosion the characteristics of animals that appeared include __.

Scientists rely on multiple lines of evidence to learn about animal phylogenetic relationships and evolutionary history. Each has benefits and drawbacks. Of the four main sources of evidence listed below which are more useful in determining the placement of ancient extinct organisms on a phylogenetic tree? 1. Fossil record 2. Comparative morphology 3. Comparative development 4. Comparative genomics

If the current molecular evidence regarding animal origins is further substantiated in the future what will be TRUE of any contrary evidence regarding the origin of animals derived from the fossil record?
Option 1: The contrary fossil evidence will be seen as a hoax.
Option 2: The fossil evidence will be understood to have been interpreted incorrectly because it is incomplete.
Option 3: The fossil record will henceforth be ignored.
Option 4: Phylogenies involving even the smallest bit of fossil evidence will need to be discarded.
Option 5: Only phylogenies based solely on fossil evidence will need to be discarded.

Question Text: According to the evidence collected so far the animal kingdom is __.
The last common ancestor of all animals was probably a __.
Choanoflagellates___.
Option 3: are a sister group to animals

Question Text: All of the following support the sponges-first hypothesis for the origin of multicellularity in animals EXCEPT __.
Option 1: a benthic lifestyle of choanoflagellates would support colonies of cells occurring together
Option 2: a sponges feeding cells share similar structure to choanoflagellates
Option 3: sponges possess genes that are involved in cell specialization
Option 4: there are older fossils of sponges than any other animal group
Option 5: ctenophores lack miRNAs while sponges have these gene regulators

Option 1: Lining the inside of the straw

Question Text: At which developmental stage should one be able to first distinguish a diploblastic embryo from a triploblastic embryo?

In lab you dissect starfish earthworms and nematodes. During one of your lab dissections you notice that you must cut through a layer of muscle just under the skin. This reveals a space in which you see that the gut is also covered by a layer of muscle. For which of the following organisms would this be TRUE? 1. Starfish 2. Earthworm 3. Nematode

Suppose a researcher for a pest-control company developed a chemical that inhibited the development of an embryonic mosquito’s endodermal cells. Which of the following would be a likely mechanism by which this pesticide works?
Option 1: The mosquito would have trouble digesting food due to impaired gut function.
Option 2: The mosquito would have trouble with respiration and circulation due to impaired muscle function.
Option 3: The mosquito wouldn’t be affected at all.

Question Text: Muscle cells among animals are a result of divergent evolutionary paths yet all animals have homologous genes for __.
Option 4: muscle derived from cells lining the coelom

Question Text: An organism that exhibits cephalization probably also __.
Which morphological trait evolved more than once in animals according to the phylogeny found in the accompanying figure?
An adult animal that possesses bilateral symmetry would most likely be __.
If an animal has a centralized nervous system it will also have __. 1. a complete gut 2. bilateral symmetry 3. radial symmetry 4. mesoderm 5. a definite head end (cephalization)
Animals with a cephalized body structure would be better able to __.
Option 2: extend a nerve net in all directions from a central point

Question Text: Which of the following is a feature of the tube-within-a-tube body plan in most animal phyla?
Option 1: The outer tube consists of a hard exoskeleton.
Option 2: The outer tube consists of digestive organs.
Option 3: The mouth and anus form the ends of the inner tube.
Option 4: The two “tubes” are separated by tissue that comes from embryonic endoderm.

Question Text: Among Bilateria which morphological trait has shown the most variation?
Option 1: Type of symmetry (bilateral versus radial versus none)
Option 2: Type of body cavity (coelom versus pseudocoelom versus no coelom)
Option 3: Number of embryonic tissue types (diploblasty versus triploblasty)
Option 4: Type of development (mouth first versus anus first versus neither)

Question Text: Among the Bilatera which of the following statements is TRUE?
Option 1: Deuterostomes have more variation in blastopore development than Protostomes.
Option 2: Deuterostomes have more variation in number of embryonic tissue types that Protostomes.
Option 3: Protostomes have more variation in type of symmetry than Deuterostomes.

Question Text: You are carefully examining a living organism. Which of the following should convince you that the organism is acoelomate?
Option 3: It possesses sensory structures at its anterior end.
Option 4: Muscular activity of its digestive system distorts the outer body wall.

Question Text: What was an early selective advantage of a coelom in animals? A coelom __.
Option 1: contributed to a hydrostatic skeleton allowing greater range of motion
Option 2: was a more efficient digestive system
Option 3: allowed cephalization and the formation of a cerebral ganglion

Question Text: In the traditional morphological phylogeny (A), the phylum Platyhelminthes is depicted as a sister taxon to the rest of the protostome phyla and as having diverged earlier from the lineage that led to the rest of the protostomes. In the molecular phylogeny (B), Platyhelminthes is depicted as a lophotrochozoan phylum. What probably led to this change?
Option 1: Platyhelminthes ceased to be recognized as true protostomes.
Option 2: Similarity of genetic information among triploblast groups has led to realization that some morphological features are evolutionarily flexible (e.g. coelom formation) and so cannot be used as a diagnostic character.
Option 3: All Platyhelminthes must have a well-developed lophophore as their feeding apparatus.
Option 4: Platyhelminthes’ close genetic ties to the arthropods became clear as their Hox gene sequences were studied.

Question Text: Use the following information.
Option 1: Pseudocoelomates can be seen as an intermediate stage between acoelomate and coelomate development.
Option 2: Although acoelomates and pseudocoelomates evolved only once coelomates evolved multiple times in different lineages.
Option 3: Only the animals that evolved earliest are acoelomates.
Option 4: Some pseudocoelomates and acoelomates have evolved from coelomates.

Question Text: New evidence indicates that the fate of the blastopore in Protostomes is variable. What is the fate of the blastopore in Deuterostomes?
Option 1: In Deuterostomes the fate of the blastopore is also variable.
Option 2: In Deuterostomes the blastopore will eventually form the mouth.
Option 3: In Deuterostomes the blastopore will eventually form the anus.

Question Text: Which of the statements regarding diversity in animals is TRUE?
Option 1: Although animal feeding strategies are very diverse animals have the same sensory abilities.
Option 2: Although animal sensory structures are very diverse animals all feed the same way.
Option 3: Although animals reproduce in different ways all animals have the same kind of development.
Option 4: Although animals can have different types of appendages all animals move under their own power at some point in their life.
Option 5: Although some animals have different kinds of development all animals use appendages to move.
